skill.name: api-documentation
skill.description: API documentation workflow for generating OpenAPI specs, creating developer guides, and maintaining comprehensive API documentation.
skill.summary: Generate comprehensive API documentation including OpenAPI specs, developer guides, code examples, and interactive documentation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security analysis confirms this is a safe documentation workflow skill. The static analyzer flagged 28 instances of 'external_commands' (Ruby/shell backtick execution) and 9 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithms', but both are false positives. The backticks in the file are Markdown code spans (e.g., `api-documenter`) used for skill name references, not shell command execution. No actual security risks were identified.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at 28 locations, but these are Markdown inline code formatting (backticks around skill names like `api-documenter`), not shell command substitution. This is a false positive.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged lines contain Markdown code spans with skill names like `api-documenter`. No shell command execution exists in this documentation-only skill file.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 9 instances of 'weak cryptographic algorithm', but this skill is a documentation workflow with no cryptographic operations. Likely triggered by pattern matching on the word 'api' or YAML content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a pure documentation workflow skill. No cryptographic algorithms, encryption, or hashing operations exist. The detection appears to be a scanner misconfiguration triggered by benign content.

content.value_statement: This workflow helps developers create comprehensive API documentation by guiding them through OpenAPI specification creation, developer guide writing, code example generation, and interactive documentation setup.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through 7 phases of API documentation workflow
Generate OpenAPI/Swagger specifications from API definitions
Create developer guides with authentication and usage patterns
Produce working code examples in multiple languages
Set up interactive documentation with Swagger UI and Redoc
limitations: Requires other specialized skills to perform specific tasks
Does not directly interact with APIs or code
Cannot generate documentation without API specifications
Does not host or deploy documentation sites

use_cases.title: New API Project Documentation
use_cases.description: Create complete documentation for a new REST API from scratch, including OpenAPI spec, developer guide, and interactive docs.

use_cases.title: Legacy API Documentation Update
use_cases.description: Update and modernize existing API documentation to include OpenAPI specs, code examples, and interactive try-it features.

use_cases.title: Internal API Portal Creation
use_cases.description: Build an internal API portal with comprehensive documentation, code samples, and integration guides for internal teams.

prompt_templates.title: Start API Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the @api-documentation workflow to create documentation for my REST API. I have an endpoint list and need help generating OpenAPI specs and a developer guide.

prompt_templates.title: Generate OpenAPI Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @api-documentation to create an OpenAPI specification for my API. The API has authentication via JWT and includes endpoints for users, products, and orders.

prompt_templates.title: Add Code Examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the @api-documentation workflow to add code examples to my existing API documentation. I need examples in Python, JavaScript, and cURL.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Interactive Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @api-documentation to set up interactive documentation with Swagger UI and Redoc for my existing OpenAPI spec. Configure try-it functionality.

output_examples.input: Use @api-documentation workflow to document my user management API
output_examples.output:
I will guide you through creating comprehensive API documentation in 7 phases:

**Phase 1: API Discovery**
- Inventory your endpoints
- Document request/response formats
- Identify authentication requirements

[Guides through each phase with specific actions]

output_examples.input: Create OpenAPI spec for my e-commerce API
output_examples.output:
Here's your OpenAPI specification structure:

```yaml
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: E-Commerce API
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /products:
    get:
      summary: List products
      responses:
        200:
          description: Successful response
```

best_practices: Start with API discovery to understand all endpoints before generating documentation
Keep OpenAPI specs in sync with actual API changes using automation
Test all code examples to ensure they work in production environments
anti_patterns: Skipping the discovery phase and jumping straight to documentation generation
Generating documentation without verifying accuracy of API specifications
Creating static docs that never get updated when APIs change

faq.question: What skills does this workflow invoke?
faq.answer: This workflow invokes skills like api-documenter, openapi-spec-generation, api-documentation-generator, docs-architect, and others to perform specific documentation tasks.

faq.question: Do I need to install other skills?
faq.answer: Yes, some phases require other skills like api-documenter, openapi-spec-generation, and documentation-templates to be available in your skill library.

faq.question: Can this workflow generate documentation for GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: This workflow is optimized for REST APIs with OpenAPI specifications. GraphQL support would require adaptation or additional skills.

faq.question: How do I keep my API documentation up to date?
faq.answer: Phase 7 covers automation setup with validation and monitoring. You can configure webhooks to trigger documentation rebuilds when APIs change.

faq.question: What output formats does this workflow support?
faq.answer: The workflow produces OpenAPI (YAML/JSON), Markdown developer guides, code examples in multiple languages, and configures Swagger UI or Redoc for interactive docs.

faq.question: Can I customize the documentation template?
faq.answer: Yes, you can modify the workflow phases and prompts to match your organization's documentation standards and branding requirements.
